evals.prompt: Send this task to the general VP: research the top 5 Python async frameworks and write a comparison summary. Save to artifacts.
evals.expected_output: Mission dispatched to vp.general.primary. User is informed of the mission_id and status. Agent waits for result via vp_wait_mission, then reads artifacts.
expectations: The first tool call in the turn is vp_dispatch_mission (not a preflight search or discovery call)
The objective field is a non-empty string describing the research task
The mission_id from the dispatch response is reported to the user
vp_wait_mission is called with the mission_id from dispatch
If the mission completes, vp_read_result_artifacts is called to surface output files

evals.prompt: Have the coder VP refactor the authentication module in /opt/projects/myapp to use async/await throughout.
evals.expected_output: Mission dispatched to vp.coder.primary. Agent includes the external path constraint and waits for completion.
expectations: The first tool call in the turn is vp_dispatch_mission
The objective describes the refactor task with the external path
The mission is NOT targeted at UA internal repo paths
An idempotency_key is included or the tool auto-generates one
vp_wait_mission is called after dispatch with a bounded timeout_seconds

evals.prompt: Dispatch a VP mission then cancel it before it completes.
evals.expected_output: Mission dispatched, then vp_cancel_mission called with a descriptive reason. User informed of cancellation status.
expectations: vp_cancel_mission is called with the mission_id from dispatch
A non-empty reason is provided to vp_cancel_mission
The cancel response ok=true is confirmed before reporting to user
If cancel returns ok=false, the error code and message are surfaced
